artifacts.sourceTitle: راهنمای استفاده از ابزارهای هوش مصنوعی در پژوهش
artifacts.queryUsed: site:iut.ac.ir هوش مصنوعی دانشگاه صنعتی اصفهان سیاست آموزشی اخلاق پژوهش
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official IUT Research and Technology page links a Persian PDF guide on use of AI tools in research. The PDF is ministry-authored, so claims are scoped to IUT hosting/availability and the guide contents rather than a university-wide IUT-only policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: هوش مصنوعی و واقعیت افزوده در آموزش و پژوهش و بحث تعاملی در اخلاق آن
artifacts.queryUsed: site:iut.ac.ir هوش مصنوعی دانشگاه صنعتی اصفهان سیاست آموزشی اخلاق پژوهش
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official IUT Educational Monitoring and Evaluation office page describes a faculty development course on AI and augmented reality in education/research, including ethical dimensions and responsible use.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:iut.ac.ir هوش مصنوعی دانشگاه صنعتی اصفهان سیاست آموزشی اخلاق پژوهش
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official research ethics page, but the retrieved content is general human/animal/biomedical research ethics guidance and was not used as AI-policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: کانال رسمی دانشگاه صنعتی اصفهان - Telegram
artifacts.verificationNotes: Telegram search result mentioned the official course URL, but social posts are outside the evidence boundary for this run.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page discusses which research requires an ethics code, proposal ethics considerations, and biomedical research ethics workflow; it does not provide AI-use guidance.
artifacts.notes: Official page retained as a rejected/limited source because it is research-policy relevant but not AI-specific.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Telegram search result referenced the official IUT course URL.
artifacts.notes: Social/channel material was not used as evidence; the official IUT page was used instead.

methodsAttempted.query: site:iut.ac.ir هوش مصنوعی دانشگاه صنعتی اصفهان سیاست آموزشی اخلاق پژوهش
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced official IUT research and educational monitoring pages.

methodsAttempted.query: official IUT AI course and research guide URLs
methodsAttempted.notes: Browser/web retrieval opened the official IUT pages and the linked official PDF text after Firecrawl and local curl timed out.

methodsAttempted.query: site:iut.ac.ir Isfahan University of Technology artificial intelligence policy students assessment academic integrity generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: English query did not surface useful official policy pages.

artifacts.summary: Official IUT evidence was limited to a research-office page hosting a Persian AI-in-research guide and an educational monitoring office faculty-development course page. No university-wide student AI assessment or academic integrity policy was found in the attempted official-source searches.

stopConditions: Stop after official IUT sources produce source-hash-backed claim/evidence candidates.
Do not use Telegram, ranking pages, media coverage, or search snippets as evidence.
Do not infer a university-wide student policy from course/training pages or ministry-authored guidance hosted by IUT.

artifacts.claimText: Isfahan University of Technology's Research and Technology site hosts a Persian PDF titled 'Guide to Using AI Tools in Research'; the linked guide addresses AI use in research and is not treated here as a university-wide IUT-only policy.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: این دوره با هدف آشنایی اعضای هیئت علمی با مفاهیم بنیادین، فرصت‌ها و چالش‌های هوش مصنوعی و واقعیت افزوده در عرصه‌های آموزش و پژوهش برگزار می‌شود ... بررسی ابعاد اخلاقی استفاده از هوش مصنوعی ... بهره‌مندی مسئولانه و مؤثر از فناوری‌های نوین در محیط دانشگاهی
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The course page says the faculty course covers AI/AR in education and research, ethical dimensions of AI use, and responsible/effective use of new technologies in the university environment.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow source-status claim only. The PDF is hosted on an official IUT research page but appears ministry-authored, so this does not assert a binding IUT-only policy.
Approved as a narrow teaching/faculty-development claim. The source is a course page, not a student conduct or assessment policy.

artifacts.title: Isfahan University of Technology AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official IUT sources found in this run support two narrow staged claims: IUT's research site hosts a Persian AI-in-research guide, and IUT's educational monitoring office announced a faculty course discussing AI ethics and responsible use in education/research. No university-wide student assessment, academic integrity, or binding institutional AI policy was found in the attempted official-source searches.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Firecrawl and direct curl fetches timed out against several IUT hosts; browser-indexed official pages were used where accessible.
The hosted AI-in-research PDF appears to be ministry-authored; the staged claim is limited to official IUT hosting/availability and guide contents.
The faculty-development course page is not a binding policy and should not be presented as a student-facing rule.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
